audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed as continuation of the whirlwind: Job's first reply, renewed divine challenge, justice/pride questions, and Behemoth imagery. Behemoth is preserved as poetic/mythic wild creature language rather than flattened.

verses.restored: The Eternal answered Job and said:
Shall one who argues with Shaddai correct him? Let the one who reproves the Divine answer it.
Job answered the Eternal and said:
Look, I am small. What shall I answer you? I place my hand over my mouth.
Once I have spoken, and I will not answer; twice, but I will add no more.
Then the Eternal answered Job from the whirlwind and said:
Gird your loins like a strong man; I will ask you, and you make it known to me.
Will you truly annul my justice? Will you condemn me so that you may be righteous?
Do you have an arm like the Divine, and can you thunder with a voice like his?
Adorn yourself now with majesty and height; clothe yourself with splendor and glory.
Pour out the overflowings of your anger; look at every proud one and bring him low.
Look at every proud one and humble him; tread down the wicked where they stand.
Hide them in the dust together; bind their faces in the hidden place.
Then I myself will acknowledge you, that your own right hand can save you.
Look now at Behemoth, which I made with you; he eats grass like an ox.
Look, his strength is in his loins, and his power in the muscles of his belly.
He bends his tail like a cedar; the sinews of his thighs are woven together.
His bones are tubes of bronze; his limbs like bars of iron.
He is the first of the ways of the Divine; only his Maker can bring near his sword.
For the mountains bring him produce, and all the beasts of the field play there.
Under lotus plants he lies, in the hiding place of reeds and marsh.
Lotus plants cover him with shade; willows of the brook surround him.
Look, if the river presses, he does not panic; he is confident though Jordan rushes to his mouth.
Can anyone take him by his eyes, or pierce his nose with snares?
